packages.name: @decantr/cli
packages.summary: Local scaffold, audit, registry, and maintenance workflows for Decantr projects.

packages.name: @decantr/core
packages.summary: Execution-pack compiler primitives and shared Decantr core utilities.

blockers: Execution-pack contracts are still expanding with hosted and mutation-centric workflows.
The public low-level integration story for @decantr/core still needs a more intentional stable API boundary.

packages.name: @decantr/css
packages.summary: Framework-agnostic CSS atom runtime for Decantr projects.

packages.name: @decantr/essence-spec
packages.summary: Essence schemas, validation, migration, and TypeScript types.

blockers: The v3/v3.1 essence evolution and compatibility policy still needs a clearly frozen stable contract.
Schema consumers still need a more explicit migration and deprecation story before stable graduation.

packages.name: @decantr/mcp-server
packages.summary: MCP delivery surface for Decantr design intelligence, packs, and verification.

blockers: The tool surface is still evolving alongside hosted fallback and verification expansion.
Auth, hosted critique, and long-term MCP contract guarantees need to settle before stable release.

packages.name: @decantr/registry
packages.summary: Registry contracts, schemas, API client, and public content utilities.

blockers: Registry content and hosted verification contracts are still being tightened across public API surfaces.
The stable browser/client contract should settle after the hosted verification and package-surface work completes.

packages.name: @decantr/verifier
packages.summary: Shared verification, critique, and report-schema engine.

blockers: Runtime verification is stronger now but still expanding toward deeper auth, security, and hosted critique workflows.
The long-term stable verifier API should settle after the next hosted verification surface lands.

packages.name: @decantr/vite-plugin
packages.summary: Experimental Vite overlay for Decantr guard feedback during local development.

blockers: The Vite overlay is still an experimental sidecar, not part of the product nucleus.
We still need to decide whether this package should graduate, remain experimental, or be archived.
